SEARCH
Module pgl.app
Package pgl.app.model

Class MapManager

java.lang.Object
pgl.app.model.MapManager
public class MapManager extends Object
Manages and orchestrates map data. Centralizes entities (Sites, UserPoints, Triangles) and synchronizes geometric calculations.
Version:
2.0

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    MapManager()
    Constructs a new MapManager with initialized empty lists for sites, user points, and triangles.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addHospital(Hospital hospital)
    Adds a reference site to the map and updates the entire map structure.
    void
    addHospitals(List<Hospital> newHospitals)
    Adds a list of sites in bulk and updates the map only once to optimize the performance of the triangulation engine.
    void
    addIncident(VictimIncident incident)
    Adds an incident point to the map, snaps it to the nearest road network intersection if available, and updates the map view.
    void

    Adds a list of incidents in bulk

    Clears all sites, user points, and triangles from the manager.

    Delegates the counting of active incidents that are currently assigned to a specific hospital to AnalyticsEngine.

    Returns an unmodifiable view of the user points list to preserve strict encapsulation.

  • Constructor Details

    • MapManager

      public MapManager()
      Constructs a new MapManager with initialized empty lists for sites, user points, and triangles.

  • Method Details

    • addHospital

      public void addHospital(Hospital hospital) throws HospitalCollisionException
      Adds a reference site to the map and updates the entire map structure.
      Parameters:
      hospital - the hospital to be added
      Throws:
      HospitalCollisionException - if a hospital already exists at the snapped location

    • addHospitals

      public void addHospitals(List<Hospital> newHospitals)
      Adds a list of sites in bulk and updates the map only once to optimize the performance of the triangulation engine.
      Parameters:
      newHospitals - the hodpitals list to import

    • removeHospital

      public void removeHospital(Hospital hospital)
      Removes a reference site from the map and updates the entire map structure.
      Parameters:
      hospital - the hospital to be removed

    • findHospitalById

      public Hospital findHospitalById(int id)

    • addIncident

      public void addIncident(VictimIncident incident)
      Adds an incident point to the map, snaps it to the nearest road network intersection if available, and updates the map view.
      Parameters:
      incident - the incident point to be added

    • addIncidents

      public void addIncidents(List<VictimIncident> newIncidents)
      Adds a list of incidents in bulk. * Automatically assigns each new incident to its nearest site.
      Parameters:
      newIncidents - the incidents' list to import

    • removeIncident

      public void removeIncident(VictimIncident incident)
      Removes a user point from the map.
      Parameters:
      incident - the user point to be removed

    • findIncidentById

      public VictimIncident findIncidentById(String id)

    • addRoad

      public RoadEdge addRoad(Point start, Point end, double trafficFactor)

    • addRoad

      public RoadEdge addRoad(int startIdx, int endIdx, double trafficFactor)

    • getRoadNetwork

      public RoadNetwork getRoadNetwork()

    • updateAll

      public void updateAll()
      Updates the entire map state by recalculating the Delaunay triangulation and updating all user assignments.

    • updateSingleUserAssignment

      public void updateSingleUserAssignment(VictimIncident incident)

    • getSites

      public Set<Hospital> getSites()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the sites list to preserve strict encapsulation.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of sites

    • getIncidents

      public List<VictimIncident> getIncidents()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the user points list to preserve strict encapsulation.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of user points

    • getTriangles

      public List<Triangle> getTriangles()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the triangles list to preserve strict encapsulation.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of triangles

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears all sites, user points, and triangles from the manager.

    • getIncidentCountForHospital

      public int getIncidentCountForHospital(Hospital hospital)
      Delegates the counting of active incidents that are currently assigned to a specific hospital to AnalyticsEngine.
      Parameters:
      hospital - The hospital to inspect.
      Returns:
      Count of assigned incidents.

    • getVoronoiCells

      public List<VoronoiCell> getVoronoiCells()
      Delegates the generation of Voronoi cells to the dedicated geometry engine.

      This method utilizes the internal VoronoiEngine to compute the geometric boundaries of each hospital's coverage zone based on the current list of hospitals and Delaunay triangles.

      Returns:
      a List of VoronoiCell objects representing the partition of the map area

    • getVoronoiNeighbors

      public Set<Hospital> getVoronoiNeighbors(Hospital hospital)

    • getRoutingEngine

      public RoutingEngine getRoutingEngine()
      Retourne le moteur de routage. S'il n'existe pas encore, il le crée. C'est ce qu'on appelle le "Lazy Loading" (Chargement paresseux).

    • getStatsForHospital

      public HospitalStats getStatsForHospital(Hospital hospital)
      Delegates statistical calculation to the stateless AnalyticsEngine.

      Computes performance and operational metrics (such as total incidents, minimum, maximum, and average response distances) for a specific medical facility.

      Parameters:
      hospital - the Hospital for which statistics are being collected
      Returns:
      a HospitalStats object containing the aggregated statistical metrics

    • getTriangleLoadImbalance

      public int getTriangleLoadImbalance(Triangle t)
      Delegates imbalance analysis to the stateless AnalyticsEngine.

      Evaluates the workload distribution discrepancy among the three hospitals forming the vertices of the specified Delaunay triangle.

      Parameters:
      t - the Triangle whose vertex hospitals are being analyzed
      Returns:
      the absolute difference between the maximum and minimum incident counts among the triangle's vertices

    • computeRoadForIncident

      public List<Point> computeRoadForIncident(VictimIncident incident)
      Computes the optimal road path from a specific incident location to its closest hospital site.

      This method instantiates a temporary RoutingEngine to calculate the shortest or most efficient route using the underlying road network infrastructure. If the incident has no assigned hospital or if the road network data is unavailable, an empty list is safely returned.

      Parameters:
      incident - the VictimIncident representing the starting point of the route
      Returns:
      a List of Point objects defining the sequential path coordinates from the incident to the hospital; returns an empty list if routing cannot be performed
